# Spec — 2026-05-11

## Executive Summary

- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (v7.2.49, ~12 commits this week): Shell command streaming stabilization in VS Code; Agent Manager tab bar UX overhaul (tooltips, left-aligned controls, separators); empty task header graph fix; no new CLI-side features beyond what shipped in yesterday's report
- **anthropics/claude-code** (v2.1.137–v2.1.138): `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` unconditional permission deny rules; MCP servers silently disappearing after `/clear` fixed; MCP OAuth refresh token race fixed; `@`-mention file picker now finds files created mid-session; plugin `Stop`/`UserPromptSubmit` hook cache-cleanup fix; unbounded MCP stdout memory growth fix (10GB+ RSS); pinned GitHub Actions to commit SHAs for supply chain security
- **cline/cline** (1 major commit this week): Filesystem-type telemetry (`fs_class`/`fs_type`) on mention-search with fire-and-forget capture; 3s outer timeout + UNKNOWN cache poisoning fix for fs-info; multi-root workspace correctly attributes telemetry to searched root rather than always primary root
- **aider-ai/aider** (recent): Claude Opus 4.7 + Sonnet 4.6 model support across Bedrock/Vertex/OpenRouter; GPT-5.5 model settings; `ExInfo` for `PermissionDeniedError` providing structured error context; model alias remapping (opus→4.7, sonnet→4.6); overeager mode for Sonnet 4.5; temperature disabled for Claude 4 models
- **continuedev/continue** (recent): Session history filtering by workspace directory; Mercury-2 diffusion model with tool-calling; unified dynamic model fetching for all providers; OpenRouter proper HTTP headers (X-OpenRouter-Title, X-OpenRouter-Categories); Winston logger stderr redirect for IPC stability
- **Key new gap since yesterday**: `autoMode.hard_deny` — Claude Code now supports unconditional permission deny rules that block regardless of user intent or allow exceptions. Our permission system has allow/deny but no "hard deny" that overrides allow rules.
- **Pattern trend**: MCP stability/resilience is the dominant theme — memory leak fixes, OAuth token races, reconnection after `/clear`, and server discovery failures are all getting attention across Claude Code. Tools are hardening against real-world MCP server misbehavior.
- **Convergence signal**: Session history scoping by workspace directory appeared in Continue — when a user works in multiple projects, listing sessions only from the current workspace. Our `SessionManager` currently returns all sessions without workspace filtering.

## What to build (prioritized)

### 1. Auto-Mode Hard Deny Permission Rules
- **What**: Add a `hard_deny` permission tier that blocks tool execution unconditionally — cannot be overridden by user intent or explicit allow rules
- **Why**: Security-critical environments need irrevocable deny rules (e.g., never write to `/etc`, never execute `rm -rf /`). Current allow/deny can be overridden by user confirmation. Claude Code shipped `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` for this exact use case.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.138 — "Added `settings.autoMode.hard_deny` for auto mode classifier rules that block unconditionally regardless of user intent or allow exceptions"
- **Category**: config
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 2. Session History Workspace Filtering
- **What**: Add optional `workspaceDirectory` parameter to session listing so users can scope session history to the current project directory
- **Why**: Users working across multiple projects see a polluted session list. Continue just shipped this exact feature. Our `SessionManager.listSessions()` returns all sessions without any workspace awareness.
- **Source**: continuedev/continue #11813 — "feat: filter session history by workspace directory"
- **Category**: command
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 3. MCP Server Memory Bound on Non-Protocol stdout
- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts`, cap the internal buffer when an MCP server writes non-JSON/non-protocol data to stdout, and emit a diagnostic warning instead of growing unbounded
- **Why**: If an MCP server's child process writes debug logs or binary data to stdout, our StdioClientTransport buffers everything waiting for valid JSON-RPC. Claude Code just fixed a 10GB+ RSS leak from this exact scenario. Our MCP client has no such protection.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.138 — "Fixed unbounded memory growth (10GB+ RSS) when a stdio MCP server writes non-protocol data to stdout"
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

### 4. MCP Server Reconnection After Session Clear
- **What**: Ensure MCP servers configured in project `.mcp.json` are re-initialized after a `/clear` command rather than silently disappearing
- **Why**: Claude Code fixed a bug where MCP servers (`.mcp.json`, plugins, connectors) silently disappeared after `/clear`. If our session clear/reset path doesn't explicitly reconnect MCP servers, the same silent loss would occur.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.138 — "Fixed MCP servers configured in `.mcp.json`, plugins, and connectors silently disappearing after `/clear`"
- **Category**: mcp
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

### 5. Structured PermissionDeniedError with Context (ExInfo Pattern)
- **What**: When a permission check denies a tool call, return a structured error object (resource, action, reason, configured rule) instead of a plain string, so the model can adapt behavior
- **Why**: Aider just added `ExInfo` to `PermissionDeniedError` to provide structured context about why a permission was denied. Our permission system returns boolean deny with a message string — no structured context for the orchestrator to reason about.
- **Source**: aider-ai/aider — "feat: Add ExInfo for PermissionDeniedError"
- **Category**: tool
- **Size**: small (< 4h)
